name: senet

members.name: SENetConfig
members.signature: SENetConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, layers: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 4, 6, 3), reduction: int = 16, block_type: str = 'bottleneck', legacy_pool: bool = False)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, layers: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 4, 6, 3), reduction: int = 16, block_type: str = 'bottleneck', legacy_pool: bool = False)

members.summary: Unified config for all SE-ResNet variants (Hu et al., 2017).
members.extended:
Paper: "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"

`block_type` selects BasicBlock (SE-ResNet-18/34) or Bottleneck
(SE-ResNet-50/101/152).  `layers` is the per-stage repetition count.
`reduction` is the channel reduction ratio in the SE block (default 16).
`legacy_pool` selects the original SENet stem max-pool geometry
(`padding=0`, `ceil_mode=True`) used by the canonical SE-ResNet
line; `False` (default) uses the modern ResNet stem pool
(`padding=1`, `ceil_mode=False`).

members.name: SENet
members.extended:
Implements the Squeeze-and-Excitation augmentation of the
ResNet topology from Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation
Networks", CVPR 2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)) — winner of the
ILSVRC 2017 classification challenge.  Each residual block in
the four-stage ResNet body is followed by a lightweight SE
module that recalibrates the relative importance of each
feature-map channel:

$$
\tilde{u}_c = s_c \cdot u_c,
\qquad s = \sigma\big(W_2 \,\delta(W_1 z)\big),
\quad z_c = \frac{1}{HW} \sum_{i, j} u_c(i, j),
$$

where $\delta$ is ReLU and $\sigma$ is the
sigmoid.  The two-layer FC bottleneck has reduction ratio
$r$ (default 16), making the SE module add only ~10%
extra parameters relative to ResNet while reducing ImageNet
top-5 error by roughly 1.5 percentage points.

Block topology follows `config.block_type`: `"basic"`
selects two-conv `_SEBasicBlock`s (SE-ResNet-18/34) and
`"bottleneck"` selects three-conv `_SEBottleneck`s
(SE-ResNet-50/101/152).

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the factory functions
(`se_resnet_18`, `se_resnet_50`, …) for
paper-cited variants.

examples:
Build an SE-ResNet-50 backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_50
>>> backbone = se_resnet_50()
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = backbone(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(2, 2048, 7, 7)
notes:
State-dict keys follow timm's `seresnet` layout
(`conv1.*`, `bn1.*`, `layer{1-4}.*`) so that pretrained
weight files round-trip without renaming.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: bn1
attributes.description: BatchNorm paired with the stem conv.

attributes.name: maxpool
attributes.description:
3×3 max-pool at stride 2 — `nn.MaxPool2d` for the modern
stem, or `_LegacyStemPool` when `config.legacy_pool`.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Per-stage descriptor (channels + reduction factor) exposed
via `BackboneMixin`.

members.name: SENetForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: SE-ResNet with global-average-pooled linear classification head.
members.extended:
Combines a `SENet` backbone with global average pooling
and a linear projection (`fc`) to `config.num_classes`
logits.  When `labels` are supplied to `forward`, a
cross-entropy loss is computed and returned alongside the
logits.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture spec.  Use the `*_cls` factory functions
(`se_resnet_18_cls` through `se_resnet_152_cls`)
for paper-cited configurations.

examples:
Run inference on a batch of 224×224 RGB images:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_50_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_50_cls()
>>> x = lucid.randn(4, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(4, 1000)
notes:
State-dict keys follow timm's `seresnet` layout
(`conv1.*`, `bn1.*`, `layer{1-4}.*`, `fc.*`) so that
pretrained weight files round-trip without renaming.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: conv1, bn1, maxpool, layer1, layer2, layer3, layer4
attributes.description:
Same backbone components as on `SENet`; see that
class for shape semantics.

attributes.name: avgpool
attributes.description:
Global average pool collapsing the final feature map to
`1 × 1`.

attributes.name: fc
attributes.description:
Linear projection from `hidden_sizes[3] * block.expansion`
(512 for BasicBlock, 2048 for Bottleneck) to
`config.num_classes`.

members.name: se_resnet_18
members.extended:
Builds an `SENet` with ResNet-18 topology
(`_SEBasicBlock` blocks stacked `[2, 2, 2, 2]`) plus
a squeeze-and-excitation module at the end of every block.
Approximately 11.8M parameters — about 1% more than plain
ResNet-18 — at a typical 1.0–1.5 point ImageNet top-1
accuracy improvement.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored — the returned model is randomly initialised.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `SENetConfig`
(e.g. `reduction=8` to tighten the SE bottleneck).

returns.description:
Backbone with the SE-ResNet-18 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_18
>>> model = se_resnet_18()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes:
See Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks", CVPR 2018
([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)), Table 2.

members.name: se_resnet_18_cls
members.signature: se_resnet_18_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: SEResNet18Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: SE-ResNet-18 image classifier (backbone + GAP + linear head).
members.extended:
Builds an `SENetForImageClassification` with the
SE-ResNet-18 backbone (`_SEBasicBlock` blocks stacked
`[2, 2, 2, 2]`) followed by global average pooling and a
linear projection to `config.num_classes` (default 1000 for
ImageNet-1k).  Approximately 11.8M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`SEResNet18Weights.IN1K`); a tag
string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `SENetConfig`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the SE-ResNet-18 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it), optionally
initialised from pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_18_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_18_cls(num_classes=10)
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(2, 10)
notes:
See Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks", CVPR 2018
([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)), Table 2.  Pretrained weights are converted
from timm's `legacy_seresnet18.in1k` and hosted on the Hugging
Face Hub under `lucid-dl/se-resnet-18`.

members.name: se_resnet_34
members.extended:
Builds an `SENet` with ResNet-34 topology
(`_SEBasicBlock` blocks stacked `[3, 4, 6, 3]`) plus
squeeze-and-excitation modules.  Approximately 21.9M
parameters.  A drop-in upgrade over plain ResNet-34 with
typical 1.0–1.5 point ImageNet top-1 gain.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `SENetConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the SE-ResNet-34 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_34
>>> model = se_resnet_34()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 512, 7, 7)
notes:
See Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks", CVPR 2018
([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)), Table 2.

members.name: se_resnet_50
members.extended:
Builds an `SENet` with ResNet-50 topology
(`_SEBottleneck` blocks stacked `[3, 4, 6, 3]`) plus
squeeze-and-excitation modules at the end of every bottleneck.
Approximately 28.1M parameters — about 10% more than plain
ResNet-50 — and 77.6% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy in Hu et al.,
2018 (Table 2) versus 76.2% for plain ResNet-50.  The most
widely deployed SE variant.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `SENetConfig`.

returns.description:
Backbone with the SE-ResNet-50 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_50
>>> model = se_resnet_50()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 2048, 7, 7)
notes:
See Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks", CVPR 2018
([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)), Table 2.  Final-stage output is 2048
channels.

members.name: se_resnet_50_cls
members.signature: se_resnet_50_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: SEResNet50Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: SE-ResNet-50 image classifier (backbone + GAP + linear head).
members.extended:
Builds an `SENetForImageClassification` with the
SE-ResNet-50 backbone (`_SEBottleneck` blocks stacked
`[3, 4, 6, 3]`) followed by global average pooling and a
linear projection to `config.num_classes`.  Approximately
28.1M parameters and 77.6% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy in
Hu et al., 2018 (Table 2) — the canonical SE variant.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`SEResNet50Weights.RA2_IN1K`); a
tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `SENetConfig`
(typically `num_classes` to retarget the classifier).

returns.description:
Classifier with the SE-ResNet-50 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it), optionally
initialised from pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.senet import se_resnet_50_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_50_cls(num_classes=10)
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(2, 10)
notes:
See Hu et al., "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks", CVPR 2018
([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)), Table 2.  Pretrained weights are converted
from timm's `seresnet50.ra2_in1k` (RandAugment recipe, 78.5
top-1) and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under
`lucid-dl/se-resnet-50`.

(`_SEBottleneck` blocks stacked `[3, 4, 23, 3]`) plus
squeeze-and-excitation modules.  Approximately 49.3M
parameters and 78.3% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy in Hu et al.,
2018 (Table 2) versus 77.4% for plain ResNet-101.

members.extended:

paper.

members.name: SEResNet18Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-18 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 11.8 M params, top-1 70.6%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_18_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_18_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

members.name: SEResNet34Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-34 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 22.0 M params, top-1 73.31%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_34_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_34_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

members.name: SEResNet50Weights
members.extended:
Sourced from timm's `seresnet50.ra2_in1k` RandAugment recipe — a
cleanly SE-augmented ResNet whose state-dict naming matches Lucid
one-for-one (identity key map).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_50_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_50_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

attributes.name: RA2_IN1K
attributes.description:
timm RandAugment-v2 ImageNet-1k checkpoint (top-1 78.498%),
sourced from `timm/seresnet50.ra2_in1k`.

members.name: SEResNet101Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-101 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 49.3 M params, top-1 78.32%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_101_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_101_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

members.name: SEResNet152Weights
members.extended: SE-ResNet-152 (Hu et al. CVPR 2018; 66.8 M params, top-1 78.66%).
examples:
>>> from lucid.models import se_resnet_152_cls
>>> model = se_resnet_152_cls(pretrained=True).eval()
notes:
Reference: Hu, Shen, Sun, *"Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks"*, CVPR
2018 ([arXiv:1709.01507](https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.01507)).

summary: SENet / SE-ResNet family — backbone + image classification.
citation: Hu, Jie, et al. "Squeeze-and-Excitation Networks." Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, 2018, pp. 7132–7141.
theory:
SENet introduces the **Squeeze-and-Excitation block**, a tiny
drop-in channel-attention module that recalibrates the relative
importance of each feature-map channel after every convolutional
stage.  The block runs in three phases.

The **squeeze** phase reduces each channel to a single scalar by
global average pooling over the spatial dimensions:



$$
z_c = \frac{1}{H \cdot W} \sum_{i=1}^{H} \sum_{j=1}^{W}
      u_c(i, j),
$$

yielding a channel descriptor $z \in \mathbb{R}^{C}$ that
summarises the global response of every channel.

The **excitation** phase passes $z$ through a small
two-layer fully-connected bottleneck with a reduction ratio
$r$ (typically 16) and a sigmoid output:



$$
s = \sigma\big( W_2 \,\delta( W_1 z ) \big),
\qquad W_1 \in \mathbb{R}^{C/r \times C}, \;
W_2 \in \mathbb{R}^{C \times C/r},
$$

where $\delta$ is ReLU and $\sigma$ is the
sigmoid.  The output $s \in (0, 1)^{C}$ is interpreted
as per-channel gating weights.

Finally the **scale** phase modulates the original feature
map channel-wise:



$$
\tilde{u}_c = s_c \cdot u_c.
$$

Plugging this block into the end of every residual unit of a
ResNet-50 (giving SE-ResNet-50) adds only ~10% extra parameters
and negligible FLOPs while reducing ImageNet top-5 error by
roughly 1.5 percentage points — a result that won the ILSVRC
2017 classification challenge.  In Lucid `SENetConfig`
parameterises every SE-ResNet variant (18 / 34 / 50 / 101 / 152)
with the same `layers` and `block_type` knobs as ResNet plus
the SE-specific `reduction` ratio.
